title: Kidney Stew
excerpt: This is a South African offal version of the well-known Middle Eastern dish, Shakshuka. Ingredients 12 lamb kidneys 65 ml butter 30 ml sunflower oil 1 large onion, cut into 1 cm cubes 10 ml cumin seeds, coarsely crushed in a mortar 10 ml plain paprika 5 ml (or to taste), dried chilli flakes 1 large potato, …
html:
This is a South African offal version of the well-known Middle Eastern dish, Shakshuka.

Ingredients

  • 12 lamb kidneys
  • 65 ml butter
  • 30 ml sunflower oil
  • 1 large onion, cut into 1 cm cubes
  • 10 ml cumin seeds, coarsely crushed in a mortar
  • 10 ml plain paprika
  • 5 ml (or to taste), dried chilli flakes
  • 1 large potato, cut into 1 cm cubes (about 325 ml)
  • 125 ml lamb or chicken stock
  • salt and pepper to taste
  • 500 ml cherry tomatoes, cut into quarters
  • 90 ml flat-leaf parsley, roughly chopped
  • 15 ml garlic, finely chopped (or to taste)
  • 6 eggs

Method

Dip the kidneys in boiling water for a minute, drain immediately and pull off the outer membrane. Now halve each kidney lengthways and cut out anything that bothers you.

Heat half the butter and all the sunflower oil in a black iron pan (or your prettiest pan) and fry the kidneys quickly until brown. Scoop out and set aside.

Fry the onion until soft and stir in the cumin, paprika and chilli flakes. Stir-fry for a minute or until fragrant and then add the potato and stock. Bring to the boil, lower the heat and simmer for about 6 – 10 minutes or until the potato cubes are soft.

Add the rest of the butter and stir-fry until the potatoes are golden brown. Now add the kidneys and season to taste with the salt and pepper. Stir thoroughly.

Add the tomatoes, parsley and garlic and stir-fry until the tomatoes are soft and saucy.

Now make 6 hollows in the mixture. Break an egg into each hollow and cover the pan with a lid or heavy foil.

Bake over low heat for about 4 – 8 minutes or until the eggs are cooked to your liking, then serve just like that in the pan.

Serves 6 with a rocket and avocado salad.

Easy!
